title: 11 Herbal Creams For Pertussis
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to help with pertussis, also known as whooping cough. This condition causes persistent coughing, which can be really tough on the body. Herbal teas, especially those made with Eucalyptus globulus, can help relieve some of the symptoms. Eucalyptus has natural decongestant properties that can help ease congestion in the chest, making it easier to breathe.  Another herb that's often used for pertussis is Echinacea purpurea. It's known for its immune-boosting properties, which can help your body fight off the infection. This can be especially helpful for people with weakened immune systems.  Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, is also sometimes used for its calming properties. A persistent cough can be really stressful, and lavender can help you relax and get some rest. This can be beneficial for people who are struggling to manage their symptoms.  Using herbal creams or teas can be a good option for people who want to try a more natural approach to treating pertussis. These remedies can be especially helpful for people who are looking for ways to manage their symptoms without relying on medication.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains cineole, a key bioactive constituent that helps relieve respiratory issues like pertussis. Cineole has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease congestion in the airways. Eucalyptus globulus also contains camphor, which has expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. The antispasmodic properties of cineole and camphor in Eucalyptus globulus creams can help relax the muscles in the airways, reducing coughing fits associated with pertussis. By reducing inflammation and easing congestion, Eucalyptus globulus creams can help alleviate symptoms of pertussis and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus essential oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Add vitamin E oil and eucalyptus essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify completely before use. Label and store the creams in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains the bioactive constituents alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, a common symptom of pertussis. The alkaloid isobutylamine in Echinacea purpurea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat the bacteria that cause pertussis. The phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea creams also have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the infection. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the infection, Echinacea purpurea creams may help to alleviate symptoms of pertussis and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Combine dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and coconut oil in a double boiler. Heat the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
In a separate bowl, melt the beeswax in a double boiler. Add the vitamin E oil and lavender oil. Stir until well combined.
Mix the infused oil mixture with the melted beeswax mixture. Pour the mixture into a cream dispenser or a small tin. Let it cool and set before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are active constituents with antimicrobial properties. These compounds help reduce the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria responsible for pertussis, thereby alleviating symptoms. The antiseptic properties of linalool also help soothe irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Linalyl acetate's antispasmodic properties can help relax airway muscles, making breathing easier. By targeting the bacteria and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia creams may help alleviate pertussis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of pertussis. The antispasmodic properties of gingerols and shogaols can also help relax the airway muscles, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Zingiber officinale creams may help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to pertussis. By reducing inflammation, relaxing airway muscles, and combating infections, Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale root powder.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of boiling water.
Add 1 tablespoon of Zingiber officinale root powder and 1 tablespoon of distilled water to the melted mixture. Stir until combined.
Remove from heat and let cool until the mixture thickens. Stir occasionally to prevent separation.
Once thickened, transfer the mixture to a clean container and store in the refrigerator. Apply a thin layer to affected areas as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams contains saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms such as coughing and congestion. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Sambucus nigra creams also have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the infection. The saponins in the cream can help to thin mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion. By reducing inflammation and fighting the bacteria that causes pertussis, Sambucus nigra creams may provide relief for those suffering from the illness.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Sambucus nigra flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Sambucus nigra flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Add the vitamin E oil and lavender oil to the strained mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden. Store the Sambucus nigra creams in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains carnosic acid, ursolic acid, and rosmarinic acid, which are the main bioactive constituents responsible for its benefits. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms. Rosmariinic acid, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including Bordetella pertussis, the causative agent of pertussis. The anti-inflammatory properties of carnosic acid and ursolic acid can also help alleviate coughing and inflammation in the respiratory tract. By reducing bacterial growth and inflammation, Rosmarinus officinalis creams may help alleviate some of the symptoms associated with pertussis.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, 2 teaspoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or in the microwave in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating.
In a separate bowl, mix the distilled water and Rosmarinus officinalis leaves. Let it sit for 10 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain the water mixture and discard the herbs. Add the shea butter and vitamin E oil to the water mixture and stir until combined.
Combine the melted wax and oil mixture with the water mixture and stir until smooth. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains the bioactive constituents limonene, linalool, and menthone. These constituents have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The menthone in Mentha x piperita creams has a decongestant effect, which can help to relieve congestion and open up airways. The limonene and linalool in the cream have antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax and calm the muscles in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. By reducing inflammation and congestion, Mentha x piperita creams can help to alleviate symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and difficulty breathing.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of glycerin, and 2 tablespoons of mentha x piperita essential oil.
Combine the distilled water and glycerin in a saucepan and heat over low heat, stirring until the glycerin dissolves.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.
Add the mentha x piperita essential oil to the cooled mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before using as a cream for pertussis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains bioactive constituents such as cineole, limonene, and beta-caryophyllene, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to combat the bacterial infection that causes pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of the symptoms. Cineole, in particular, has been shown to have a bactericidal effect on Bordetella pertussis, the bacterium responsible for the disease. The anti-inflammatory properties of the cream also help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. By reducing the bacterial load and inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia creams may help to alleviate the symptoms of pertussis.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Once the mixture is melted, add the distilled water and stir well.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add the vitamin E oil and Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a heat-proof container and let it cool completely before use. This cream can be applied topically to help soothe and calm the skin affected by pertussis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhetic acid, a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory properties. This anti-inflammatory effect can help reduce the inflammation of the airways associated with pertussis, making it easier for patients to breathe. The licorice root extract in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams also contains flavonoids and polyphenols, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the disease. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can also help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing the severity of coughing fits associated with pertussis. The saponins in the cream can also help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and reduce congestion.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 2 cups of glycerin, 1 cup of glycyrrhiza glabra root extract, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the distilled water in a saucepan until it reaches 180°F. Remove from heat and add glycerin, glycyrrhiza glabra root extract, and beeswax. Stir until beeswax melts.
Let the mixture cool until it reaches 120°F. Add vitamin E oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean container. Let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solidified, your glycyrrhiza glabra cream is ready to use. Store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia creams contains the bioactive constituents alkylamides, glycosides, and caffeic acid derivatives. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms. The glycosides in Echinacea angustifolia, specifically echinacoside and iso-echinacoside, have been shown to stimulate the production of interferon and activate immune cells, such as macrophages and natural killer cells. Alkylamides, like cichoric acid, have been found to have mucolytic properties, which can help break down mucus and make it easier to expel. By reducing inflammation and enhancing immune function, Echinacea angustifolia creams may provide relief from the coughing and respiratory distress associated with pertussis.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea angustifolia herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea angustifolia root and 1 cup of coconut oil in a clean bowl.
Combine 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a separate bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler until it reaches 160°F to 180°F.
Add the dried Echinacea angustifolia root to the heated coconut oil and let it steep for 4 to 6 hours.
Strain the mixture, then combine it with the beeswax and shea butter mixture. Pour the mixture into a mold and let it cool and harden.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which give it anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the inflamed skin and airways associated with pertussis. The triterpenoid saponins, specifically, have been shown to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. The antimicrobial properties of flavonoids and carotenoids help to combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate pertussis symptoms. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Calendula officinalis creams may provide relief from the symptoms of pertussis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for pertussis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax to the coconut oil and stir until melted. Remove from heat.
Add the Calendula officinalis flowers to the mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Add the vitamin E oil to the mixture and pour into small containers. Let the mixture cool and solidify before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent pertussis, also known as whooping cough. Some herbal teas, like echinacea and yarrow, have antimicrobial properties that can boost the immune system. However, there's limited research on the effectiveness of these teas in preventing pertussis. More studies are needed to confirm their benefits.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can't replace medical treatment for pertussis. If you're taking them every day, be aware that some herbs might interact with medications or worsen symptoms. Also, the effectiveness of herbal teas in treating pertussis hasn't been scientifically proven. It's essential to be cautious and monitor your body's response.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may help alleviate pertussis symptoms, but it's essential to note that they are not a cure. Some herbal teas, like thyme and eucalyptus, may start showing results within 24 to 48 hours. This can be seen in reduced coughing and fever. However, it may take longer to notice significant improvements in overall health.
faq_time: For pertussis, drinking herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon can be helpful. This is because your body is more active during these times, and the tea's ingredients can be absorbed more easily. It's thought that this can help your body fight off the infection and reduce symptoms.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those used to treat pertussis (whooping cough). Certain herbs like ginkgo biloba and St. John's Wort can speed up how the body processes these medications, making them less effective. This interaction can also change how medications work in the body, potentially causing side effects.
faq_pregnant: While some herbal teas can help with congestion, it's not clear if they can treat pertussis, a contagious respiratory infection. During pregnancy, it's especially important to be cautious with herbal teas. Some may interact with other medicines or cause problems for the baby. It's best to avoid them until the infection passes.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and treating pertussis with herbal teas, it's essential to be cautious. Some herbal teas may not be safe for you or your baby. For example, peppermint and sage can lower milk supply, while echinacea can interact with medications. Always check the ingredients and potential risks before consumption.
title_2: 11 Soothing Herbal Creams to Help Infants with Pertussis
